arts.ttl: Champions League live blog: Real Madrid-Atlético, PSV-Arsenal
arts.sum: Enjoy live updates from Tuesday's Champions League action as Real Madrid take on Atlético Madrid, PSV take on Arsenal and Dortmund take on Lille.

arts.ttl: Why are Arsenal wearing white shorts against PSV in the Champions League?
arts.sum: The Gunners are in an alternative look for their Champions League clash

arts.ttl: Arsenal dream line-up after Isak transfer as Arteta faces selection dilemma
arts.sum: However, if Isak is signed and available, the Germany international may find it difficult to start in the centre forward role. Isak through the middle as the centre forward may give Arteta a selection dilemma when it comes to one player, though.

arts.ttl: Arne Slot's X-rated Liverpool Premier League title outburst amid Arsenal battle
arts.sum: Liverpool manager Arne Slot threatened to blame Michael Oliver if his side don't win the Premier League title amid their battle with Arsenal, following the 2-2 draw with Everton at Goodison Park. "In this exchange, Slot once again shook the referee’s hand and said: 'If we don’t win the league, I’ll f****** blame you.' It is then further alleged that Slot turned to the assistant referee and shouted twice that it was 'f****** disgrace.'

arts.ttl: Liverpool handed fresh injury concern as Arsenal eye Premier League title battle
arts.sum: It marks the latest injury scare for the league leaders as Arsenal look to chase down the double-digit gap and earn a first Premier League crown in over 20 years. Arsenal's Premier League rivals Liverpool have been handed a concern as Dutch forward Cody Gakpo was absent from their Champions League open training session.

arts.ttl: Matheus Cunha to Arsenal transfer update as ‘doubts’ emerge over £62.5m move
arts.sum: Arsenal are reportedly having doubts about a potential summer move for Wolves start Matheus Cunha, following his red card against Bournemouth. The goal he scores today belongs not in the FA Cup, it belongs in the Champions League it’s that good.

arts.ttl: Arsenal handed dream transfer update as target made available and £58m asking price slashed
arts.sum: Arsenal are once again looking to bolster their squad in the summer with a world-class winger potentially available from German giants Bayern Munich. This summer could finally be the time for Arteta to make a deal with the Frenchman falling out of favour at the Allianz, and it has now been reported that the club are looking to let him go for a fraction of the price they were previously chasing.

arts.ttl: 'No other club' - Joshua Kimmich given Arsenal transfer verdict amid Bayern Munich exit talks - football.london
arts.sum: However, Kimmich now has a matter of months remaining on the deal he signed in 2021 and is free to negotiate a pre-contract agreement with clubs outside of Germany. Bayern Munich star Joshua Kimmich has been urged to snub both Arsenal and Liverpool in order to stay at the Bundesliga club beyond this season.

arts.ttl: Lewis-Skelly and Partey return, Merino remains up top - Arsenal predicted XI vs PSV Eindhoven - football.london
arts.sum: Myles Lewis-Skelly and Thomas Partey are both predicted to start for Arsenal in their Champions League match against PSV Eindhoven Lewis-Skelly and Partey return, Merino remains up top - Arsenal predicted XI vs PSV Eindhoven

arts.ttl: Arsenal sent clearest Alexander Isak transfer message yet as huge summer contract statement made
arts.sum: “You would rather have people talking about players than not in the sense we know he is a world-class player and he is somebody, obviously, other clubs would covet because he is an incredible player,” Eales said. The Newcastle United striker is expected to be a top target for clubs across Europe over the summer with Mikel Arteta's need for a new centre forward more obvious than ever.
